source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/MixtureAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D5 LOOSE smoke oracle (self-computed from the ported C++ core, NOT yet emitter-verified against the real RMC.BestFit; D6 tightens to exact). MixtureAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a 2-component Normal MixtureModel, then assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the exceedance grid. Values reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python (identical compiled core + bit-exact Mersenne Twister). thinning_interval pinned to 1 (the bayes_normal / univariate_analysis_smoke proven bit-identical path; the default thinning=20 exposes a documented population-sampler divergence). Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). Parameter layout [w1, w2, mu1, sigma1, mu2, sigma2]; rmse over a mixture inverse-CDF grid can be non-finite and is deliberately NOT asserted. Tolerances are intentionally loose (rel 1e-5).
